publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-04-13 23:46:10 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Microfilmed papers of Willard D. Straight consist of personal and official correspondence, both original and typescript copies, and reports chiefly concerning his association with the Chinese Imperial Customs Service (1902-04). Documented is his employment with Reuters during the Russo-Japanese War (1904-05); with the United States Department of State in Korea and Manchuria (1904-09); and with Edward H. Harriman, Jacob H. Schiff, and J.P. Morgan and Company (1904-09).

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Willard D. Straight was born on January 31, 1880 in Oswego, New York. His father died in 1886; the following year Straight and his family moved to Japan. In 1890 his mother died and he returned to Oswego. He attended Bordentown Military Institute in New Jersey, 1896-97, and majored in architecture at Cornell University, 1897-1901. In November 1901 he was appointed to a position with the Chinese Imperial Maritime Customs Service, and from 1902-04 he was personal secretary and assistant to Sir Robert Hart, Inspector General of the Service in Peking. Also in 1902, he illustrated Verse and Worse for J.O.P. Bland. He served as a Reuters war correspondent during the Russo-Japanese War in 1904-05, traveling to Japan and Korea in 1904 and China in 1905. That June he was appointed Vice-Consul under Edwin V. Morgan in Seoul, Korea. The consulate closed in November, and Straight was appointed to the same post under Morgan in Havana, Cuba. In June 1906 he was appointed Consul-General at Mukden, Manchuria. In November 1907 he met William Howard Taft in Vladivostok to discuss possible investments in China. In 1909 he illustrated Housboat Days in China, again for Bland. That June he left the consular service to represent J.P. Morgan and Company and other banks and investors, the American Group. The next year he negotiated Chinese currency reform and an industrial development loan for the American group.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: . In 1911, Straight married Dorothy Payne Whitney. They left China in 1912 when revolution broke out, returning to the United States. Straight continued his association with J.P. Morgan and also continued to encourage investment in China through the American Asiatic Association, of which he was elected president in 1913. In 1914, the Straights began publication of The New Republic. In 1915 Straight resigned from J.P. Morgan and was appointed third vice president of American International Corporation, work which took him to Europe in 1916. In the summers of 1915 and 1916 he attended U.S. Army training camps, strongly supporting the Preparedness Movement. He enlisted in the U.S. Army in 1917. In 1918, while arranging for the arrival of the American Peace Mission in Paris, Straight contracted pneumonia, and died on December 1, 1918.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: Compiled by:
defitem.item: Marcia HopsonIngeborg WaldElaine EngstPaticia H. Gaffney

p.emph: Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.

odd.head: General
odd.p: A portion of this collection has been microfilmed. The microfilm (rolls 1-12) for the collection has been digitized and can be viewed here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/22047.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Willard Dickerman Straight papers, #1260. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Microfilmed papers of Willard D. Straight consist of personal and official correspondence, both original and typescript copies, and reports chiefly concerning his association with the Chinese Imperial Customs Service (1902-04). Documented is his employment with Reuters during the Russo-Japanese War (1904-05); with the United States Department of State in Korea and Manchuria (1904-09); and with Edward H. Harriman, Jacob H. Schiff, and J.P. Morgan and Company (1904-09).

Letters and reports address in particular the interest of Theodore Roosevelt and William Howard Taft in the Harriman-Schiff-Morgan proposals for investments in China from 1907-09; and concern Japanese diplomacy and finance, 1909-10; Straight's interest in THE NEW REPUBLIC and the American Asiatic Association; his role in the "preparedness" campaign in New York through the Mayor's Committee on National Defense (1915-17); his connection with the American International Corporation (1916-18);

his conduct of joint purchasing operations for the French and British governments through J.P. Morgan (1915-16); his efforts to establish the merit system in the State Department career service; and his administration of the War Risk Insurance Bureau and the establishment of its headquarters overseas (1918). In addition, there are many letters to his wife, with comments on the Red Cross, U.S. Army promotional policies, Pershing as a commander, and the American Expeditionary Force.

The collection also includes his diaries (1901-18), with transcriptions, reports, and other papers about the development of railroads, waterways, and coal mines in China and Manchuria, currency reform in China, the proposed international loan to China (1909-11), and the Far Eastern situation in general. There are also books of clippings on the Russo-Japanese War and other events in the Orient, and on the World War I work of Mr. and Mrs. Straight.
Also many letters of condolence, memorials and memoirs written at the time of Straight's death, and correspondence and reports about the building of Willard Straight Hall at Cornell (1919-22).

Non-microfilmed papers include documents on his parents and genealogy; Bordentown Military Institute; military training; Cornell; George Collingwood Bennett, Straight's valet, including photographs and memorabilia; tributes, publications, drafts, inventories and seances following Straight's death; correspondence; and a diary.

Also included are photographs and photograph albums, postcards, and commercial views; published and unpublished watercolors; pen, pencil, and crayon sketches; and charcoals by Willard Straight. This includes student work such as architectural drawings, watercolors, life drawings, and sketches for the CORNELLIAN and the WIDOW, two Cornell newspapers. Also, artwork collected by the Straights, certificates, and maps; and a brass Chinese lock.
Also one leather notebook embossed in gold letters: "Major W. D. Straight."

Article from the Toronto Sunday World, Oct. 13, 1912, "How I escaped the Chinese bandits", chronicling Dorothy Whitney's escape from looters at the Pierpont Morgan compound in Peking during a recent uprising.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the published finding aid. (5MB PDF)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The microfilm (rolls 1-12) for the collection has been digitized and can be viewed here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/22047.
Digitized microfilm (rolls 1-12) also located on DVD-740-743

did.unittitle: Documents concerning his parents and genealogy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes biographies of Willard Straight, his parents, Henry H. and Emma Dickerman Straight; Willard Straight's war record, writings of Henry H. Straight, obituary of Emma D. Straight, and school reports of Willard A. Dickerman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter describing Straight family as well as a notebook ad letters describing the Needham-Crombie genealogy.

did.unittitle: Historical Sketches Relating to the First Quarter Century of the State Normal and Training School at Oswego, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes remarks on Henry H. Straight, Emma D. Straight, and friend Margaret W. Morley, 1888.

did.unittitle: Sketchbook and sketches--copybook of Emma D. Straight.
Scrapbook on Louis Agassiz--assembled by Henry H. Straight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters among family members and from Willard Straight to family members and others concerning family matters.

did.unittitle: Stamp Album--partially filled by Willard Straight.
Bordentown--printed views of campus, grade reports, and measurements of Willard Straight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Magazine of Bordentown Military Institute for 1896, 1897, 1900; includes articles by Willard Straight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, sketches, writings about Alpha Omega society at Bordentown.

did.unittitle: Memorabilia--student handbook and dance card for 1899 Easter party.
Publications--includes copy of Cornell Era, and Willard Straight's drawings for publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes of History of Art with Olaf Brauner; grade reports from Cornell and Oswego High School.

did.unittitle: The Annual of the College of Architecture, 1897-1898

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of Willard Straight's drawings.

did.unittitle: The Annual of the College of Architecture, 1898-1899.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of Willard Straight's drawings.

did.unittitle: A Book of Designs sn Drawings, College of Architecture, 1901, annotated.
Details of Building Construction--includes typed notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes Willard Straight's activities at Cornell and includes photos of classmates, friends, and campus scenes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes Willard Straight's activities during his junior year at Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Russo-Japanese War, includes notes on Japanese and Russian military strength and Straight's wardrope list.

did.unittitle: Notes--includes a sketch by Ellen B. Straight
Clippings--describe events during the Russo-Japanese War.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describe rebellion in early 1912 during which the Straight's home in Peking was ransacked.

did.unittitle: The Toronto Sunday World, Oct. 13, 1912, article "How I escaped the Chinese bandits"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes Dorothy Whitney's escape from looters at the Pierpont Morgan compound in Peking during a recent uprising.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describe Far Eastern events from 1902-1912 with letters describing clipping services from which many of the clippings came.

did.unittitle: Duplicate Correspondence--carbon copies in bound album
Duplicate Correspondence--one bound volume, carbon copies.
Clippings--one bound volume, Japan in Korea, c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One bound volume, Russo-Japanese War; Battle of Heikoutai to Anti-Peace Demonstrations in Tokyo, February-September 1905.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Polo in China," "Edward Henry Harriman," "Personal Impressions of Edward Henry Harriman," "The Heathen Invasion" by Claude Bragdon, Russian railroad timetable.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Diary of the Revolution," reprints from the Hankow Daily News from Oct. 10-Nov.28, 1911, 2 volumes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written from China, Japan, and Korea, including sketches on the letters; three photographs; and a postcard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes checks and check stubs, 1912, n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes receipts from 1907-1912, checks, and two items in Chinese.

did.unittitle: Greeting Cards and Calling Cards--from China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Described by Diane Perushek, Curator of the Wason Collection, Olin Library, 1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reuters byline, interview referring to military and railroads, 1904-1905

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Depiction of battle, revolutionary poster, prints of Buddhist Pantheons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Protest against paying Chinese taxes, 1907.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Invitation, manuscript about railroad contract, calling cards, notes, receipt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal, includes various envelopes and Straight's calling cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Speech by Herbert Croly announcing plan to publish The New Republic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The New Republic Idea" by Herbert Croly, program from banquet celebrating first anniversary of founding of the magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary contains miscellaneous notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For dinner of Cornell Architects in New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collected by Willard Straight, probably a review of a work by Jean Francois Fouquet in 1729.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior and interior photographs of Willard and Dorothy Straight's home, including an article in Harper's Bazaar on the house.

did.unittitle: Invitations, safe-conduct pass, floor plan sketches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings describe activities of Whitney, Michael, and Beatrice Straight after their father's death.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolution of support for President and war effort.

did.unittitle: Music composed by Willard Straight--annotated by Dorothy Straight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: Book from Camp of Instruction, August 10-September 6, 1915.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General orders, Governor's Island, New York; Ft. Sill, Oklahoma.

scopecontent.p: Notes, manuals, orders, and references to books, from France, 1917-1918.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain diary entries and notes on military training.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: Sketches and plans for monument and cemetery grounds at Suresnes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Detailing cost of cemetery beautification, mostly in French.

scopecontent.p: Detail events throughout Straight's life.

scopecontent.p: Notes on biography, list of people who sent letters to the Straights, lists of Straight's sketches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For biography: biographical information, lists of places visited and friends, transcription of grades from Bordentown Military Institute and Cornell, list of people to whom copy of biography was sent, outline of events in Willard Straight's life.

did.unittitle: Transcribed letters of thanks for copies of the biography sent to friends from Dorothy.
Original letter of thanks for biography from Warwick Greene.

scopecontent.p: Programs and cards from exhibitions of Straight's artwork at the Arden Gallery in Manhattan and the Pratt Institute in Brooklyn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Given to Willard Straight by Prof. Henry Morse Stephens.

Series VII George Collingwood Bennett Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns activities before Bennett came to America with some mention of the Straights and military service.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two notes from Bennett to Straight, and mentions military service and returen to America following World War I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns Willard Straight biography, distribution of copies, plans for Willard Straight Hall, list of objects in cornerstone of Willard Straight Hall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two letters from friends of Straight to Herbert Croly praising the biography.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions his photography work and contains condolences on death of Herbert Croly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mention of Bennett's marriage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions reunion of Willard Straight A.E.F. Association, coronation of King George VI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mention of Willard Straight A.E.F. Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with servicemen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Foster M. Coffin of Cornell University on tentative plans for 25th anniversary of Willard Straight Hall; Charles Vevier on the use of the Straight papers for his thesis research; Louis D. Froelick, Henry P. Fletcher, and George D. Marvin on Vevier's research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Charles Vevier, George Marvin, and Louis Froelick; cataloging of Straight's Chinese Art; 25th anniversary of Willard Straight Hall celebration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes display of Straight's photos for annual meeting of Willard Straight A.E.F. Association; and 25th anniversary of Willard Straight Hall, including writings reflecting on the event, notes, postcards, programs, and list of photos exhibited.

Series VIII Willard Straight Post of American Legion Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four volumes including meeting notices, correspondence, and financial records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two volumes including meeting notices, correspondence, clippings, and financial records.

did.unittitle: Link to a copy of the visual material finding aid and photograph index. Includes artwork, maps, certificates, textiles, photographs, and also some additional collection information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Handle albums with care, some have broken bindings or loose covers.

did.unittitle: Peking photographs (note by George D. Marvin)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of William J. Calhoun, Maurice Casenave, Cecil Davis, Richard Davis, Henry P. Fletcher, Charles French, Thomas J. Gatrell, Edwin V. Morgan, Gordon Paddock, Dorothy W. Straight, as well as Willard D. Straight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits and photographs taken in New York, Korea and Manchuria.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Negatives from box 69 combined with box 68. Negatives from box 71 combined with box 70.

did.unittitle: Modern negatives of original prints in collection
